name: python3-tabulate
title: python3-tabulate
description:
pretty-print tabular data in Python3
Pretty-print tabular data in Python, a library and a command-line utility.
The main use cases of the library are:
.
* printing small tables without hassle: just one function call, formatting is
guided by the data itself
* authoring tabular data for lightweight plain-text markup: multiple output
formats suitable for further editing or transformation
* readable presentation of mixed textual and numeric data: smart column
alignment, configurable number formatting, alignment by a decimal point
.
This package installs the library for Python 3, and the cli tool.

arguments.description: use the first row of data as a table header
arguments.raw: --header  use the first row of data as a table header

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use tabulate because pretty-print tabular data in Python3.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use tabulate as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tabulate [options] [FILE ...]` as documented upstream for tabulate.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair tabulate with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `tabulate --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: use the first row of data as a table header

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install python-tabulate. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `tabulate [options] [FILE ...]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: pretty-print tabular data in Python3.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: pretty-print tabular data in Python3.
